titleStmt.title: Vatican City, Biblioteca Apostolica Vaticana, Aeth. 124
Homiliary in honour of St Michael

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msItem.title: Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: : Introduction
msItem.note: The text corresponds to No item: BAVet82, could not check for p1_i1.1.

msItem.title: Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily by Timothy (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT1637Homily)
msItem.note: The supplication to the scribe and the owner is followed by magic names: ኤኮስ፤ አስሌ፤ ኡፓ፤ ኡፓስ፤.

msItem.title: Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Introduction to the miracles performed by Michael
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle on the Egyptian sailors (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7170MiracleMichaelSailors)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice on Michael's appearance to Joshua
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Salām for Ḫǝdār
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily on Dǝrātewos and Tewobǝstǝyā (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT3958Homily)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle on the poor peasant who found a treasure in a fish (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7171MiracleMichaelTreasure)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice for about the three youths in the furnace (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7195MiracleMichaelYouths)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: to you Saints (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT6822SalamLakaAqabi)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily on the Archangel Michael by Epiphanius (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT3959Homily)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle about the rich man who became poor (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7172MiracleRichPoor)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice on St Michael's appearance to Jacob
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: to you Michael, one of eldest highest, servant of the Lord
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily on a poor man of great faith (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT3960Homily)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle of the healing of a paralytic (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7191MiracleMichaelParalytic)
msItem.note: The text corresponds to No item: BAVet82, could not check for p1_i1.13.4, for the month of Ṭǝqǝmt.

msItem.title: Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice on St Michael's appearance to Samson
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: I say salām to your ordered beauties
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily by a patriarch of Antioch on the Psalm of David
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle on the woman suffering from elephantiasis (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7175MiracleMichaelElephantiasis)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice on St Michael's appearance to Balaam
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Salām to whom it has priority
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily by Rǝtuʿa Hāymānot (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT3962Homily)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle on a Christian whose hand had become dry (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7179MiracleMichaelHand)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice on Jeremiah freed by St Michael (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/NAR0211MichaelJeremiah)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Salām to you, whom he established
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily on Eudoxia and Yoḥannǝs (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT3963Homily)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle on the sterile woman (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7192MiracleMichaelSterile)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice on St Michael's appearance to Habakkuk (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/NAR0212MichaelHabakkuk)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Salām for your existence
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily by Yoḥannǝs on Astaraniqos and Euphemia (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT3964Homily)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle on the healing of the leprous Jew (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7180MiracleMichaelJew)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemoration on the conversion of the temple of Alexandria (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/NAR0213MichaelTemple)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Salām to Michael the Merciful (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT6631SalMikMahari)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily on Talāson by Yoḥannǝs (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT3981Homily)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle on a man freed from an evil spirit (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7181MiracleMichaelFreed)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice on St Michael's intervention against King Sennacherib (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/NAR0208MichaelSennacherib)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Salām to you helper to the holy and martyrs
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Anonymous homily referring to David's sayings on St Michael and St Michael's deeds to Lamech, Sem and Christ. (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT3966Homily)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle on the healing of a blind man (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7183MiracleMichaelBlind)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice on Michael's appearance to king Constantine (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/NAR0210MichaelConstantine)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Salām to you, messenger of the Covenant and Counsel of the Most High God (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT4035SenkessarDL#Naha12MikaelSalam)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily on the Lordship of God (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT3967Homily)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle on Dorotheus and Theophista, who found a treasure in the belly of a fish (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7185MiracleMichaelDorotheus)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice on Michael's appearance to Isaiah
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Salām to your pedestal
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel:
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Homily 'Who did not seek the soul' (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT3968Homily)
msItem.explicit: ሑሩ፡ ውስተ፡ እሳት፡ ዘለዓለም፡ ኀበ፡ አልባቲ፡ ሙፃእ፡ ወምጉያይ፡ እስከ፡ ለዓለም፨ አባግዕሰ፡ የዋሐን፡ ወትሑታታን፡ ንዑ፡ ኀቤየ፡ ወአነ፡ እሁበክሙ፡ ክብረ፡ ወመንግሥተ፨ ወትሄልው፡ ምስሌየ፡ በብዙኅ፡ ትፍሥሕት፡ ወኢይኄሱ፡ ወኢይዌልጥ፡ ቃልየ፡ ዘወጽአ፡ እምአፉየ፤ እስከ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን፨

msItem.title: Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Miracle on the poor who sent the money to the church custodian inside a fish (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/LIT7174MiracleMichaelMoneyFish)
msItem.note: The text corresponds to No item: BAVet82, could not check for p1_i1.5.2, for the month of Yakkātit.

msItem.title: Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Commemorative notice on Michael's appearance to Samuel (same as https://betamasaheft.eu/NAR0209MichaelSamuel)
Dǝrsāna Mikāʾel: Salām to you, bird of the garden of the East
History of the consecration of the church of St Michael on an island near Alexandria
msItem.note: The text corresponds to No item: BAVet82, could not check for ms_i4, where, however, the church is dedicated to the Archangel Raphael.
msItem.incipit: በስመ፡ አብ፡ አመ፡ ፫ሱ፡ ለጳጕሜን፡ ተዝካራ፡ በአሉ፡ ለሊቀ፡ለሊቅስ፡ መላእክት፡ ሚካኤል፡ በረከቱ፡ የሐሉ፡ እምሊቃነ፡ መለእክት፡ ትጉሃን፡ ቅዱሳን፡ ሰማይውያን፨ ወቅዳሴ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ እንተ፡ ተሐንፀ፡ ሎቱ፡ ዲበ፡ ደሴት፨ በአፍአ፡ ሀገረ፡ እስክንድርያ፡ በመዋዕሊሁ፡ ለአበ፡ ቴዎፍሎስ፡ ሊቀ፡ ጳጰሳት፡ ወትእምርተ፡ ዘአስተርአየ፡ ውስቴታ፡
msItem.explicit: ወነበረት፡ ይእቲ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ ከመዝ፡ እስከ፡ ነግሡ፡ ተንባለት፡ ወእምድኅረ፡ ነግሡ፡ ተንባላት፡ ተንህለት፡ ይእቲ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ በተሐውኮ፡ ዓንበሪ። ወካዕበ፡ ተሐውኮ፡ አንበሪ፡ ባሕር፡ ወአስጠሞሙ፡ ለብዙኃን፡ ሰብእ፡ እለ፡ ይነብሩ፡ መልዕልተ፡መልዕኵ፡ ውእቱ፡ መካን፡ በረከቱ፡

msItem.title: Salām-hymn to St Raphael
History of how John Chrysostom visited the church of St Michael on an island
msItem.note: The text is acephalous. The text corresponds to No item: BAVet82, could not check for ms_i5, where, however, the church is dedicated to the Archangel Raphael.
msItem.incipit: ዘሀገረ፡ ቍስጥንጥንያ፡ ለአኖሬዎስ፡ ጻድቅ፡ ወይቤሎ፡ አእምር፡ ኦንጉሥ፡እንጉሥ፡ እስመ፡ ንህነ፡ ተፅዕነ፡ ዲበ፡ ሐመር፡ ከመ፡ ንምጻእ፡ ኀቤከ፡ ወእንዘ፡ ሀሎነ፡ ነሐውር፡ ርኢነ፨ አሐተ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ ውስተ፡ ደሴት፡ በዕለተ፡ ቀደሚት፡ ሰንበት፨ ወበጻሕነ፡ ውስተ፡ መርሶ፡ ከመ፡ ንትመጦ፡ንትመጠ፡ ምሥጢረ፡ ቅድሳት፡ ሥጋሁ፡ ወደመ፨ ለክርስቶስ፡ በዕለተ፡ እሁድ፡ ወረከብነ፡ ደብረ፡ ንዑሰ፡ በገቦሃ፡ ለይእቲ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን።
msItem.explicit: አነ፡ እተነብል፡እትነበብል፡ ኀበ፡ እግዚአብሔር። በእንቲአሆሙ፡ ወአድኅኖሙ፡ እምንዳቤሆሙ፡ ወኢይሬእዩ፡ ግሙራ፡ ኵነኔ፡ ወዘንተ፡ ብሂሎ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡ ሊቀ፡ መለእክት፡ ሰገደ፡ ቅድመ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ወብዙኃት፡ ተአምራቲሁ፡ ለዝንቱ፡ መልአከ፡ ሚካኤል፡ ሊቀ፡ መላእክት፡ ወይደልወነ፡ ከመ፡ ንግበር፡ ተዝካሮ፡ ኵሎ፡ ጊዜ፡ እስመ፡ ውእቱ፡ ይትነብል፡ይትነበብ፡ ኀበ፡ እስመ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ በእንቲአነ፡ ትንብልና፡ ጸሎቱ፡

msItem.title: Salām to Melchizedek
msItem.incipit: ሰለም፡ ለመልከ፡ ጼዴቅ፡ አምሳሉ፡ ወሱታፉ፨ ለዘይመጽእ፡ ቃል፡

msItem.title: Malkǝʾa Mikāʾel

collation.signatures: Quires marks are written with Ethiopic numerals in the upper corner of the recto of the first folio of quires from to .

handDesc.handNote:
19th-century script
Habta Giyorgis is mentioned as the scribe in the supplication on . The cataloguers do not provide further information on the portion of text copied by him. Headings and holy names.

19th-century script
Walda Takla Hāymānot is mentioned as the scribe in the supplication on . The cataloguers do not provide further information on the portion of text copied by him. Headings and holy names.

decoNote.desc: Polychrome miniature of St Michael with Ethiopian clothes (violet, yellow, red).
Uncoloured drawing of St Michael. ዝኩሰ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡ ሥዩም፡ በኀበ፡ እግዚኡ፡ ምእመን።

item.desc: The note is a short supplication written down by the scribe of the main text, Habta Giyorgis. Another hand added the name of another individual, Gabra Śǝllāse, possibly a later owner of the manuscript.
item.q: ኦአምላክ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡ ዕቀበኒ፡ ወአድኅነኒ፡ ለጸሐፊሁ፡ ሀብተ፡ ጊዮርጊስ። ለገብርከ፡ ገብረ፡ ሥላሴ።

item.desc: The note is a short supplication written down by another scribe, Walda Takla Hāymānot. The note also mentions the owner of the book Walda ʾIyasus, indicated as "your servant", and his son Gabra Wald. Another hand added the name of another individual, Gabra Śǝllāse, possibly a later owner of the manuscript.
item.q: ኦአምላክ፡ ገብርኤል፡ ዕቀቦ፡ ለገብርከ፡ ወልደ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ወምስለ፡ ውሉዱ፡ ገብረ፡ ወልድ፡ ወምስለ፡ ጸሐፊሁ፡ ወልደ፡ ተክለ፡ ሃይማኖት፡ ለገብርከ፡ ገብረ፡ ሥላሴ፡

item.desc: Names of the Archangel Michael The text concludes abruptly as follows: ወትብል፡ በትሁት፡ ቃል፡ እሎንተ፡ አስማተ፡ ዓበይተ፡ አክሶ፡ ለሜን፡ ለኬ፡ ኤንክ፡ ካዜእ፡ አርናኬ፡ ኬንኬ። ብአሳ፡ ፌኤ፡ ኤልካ፡.
A violet stamp is found on .

origin.note: The manuscript was written by at least two scribes, Habta Giyorgis (mentioned on ) and Walda Takla Hāymānot (mentioned on ). The manuscript belonged to several inviduals, whose names are mentioned throughout the text. The name of Walda ʾIyasus occurs on , together with that of his son Gabra Wald on . The name of another owner, that of Gabra Śǝllāse, is sometimes inserted in a rudimentary hand, mostly interlineally, except on , where a blank space had been left. His name is also added in a secondary hand in the supplications on . Finally, another name, that of Takla Māryām, is found in the lower margin of .

history.provenance: According to the account on the manuscripts’ provenance given by Eugène Tisserant, this manuscript is among those acquired by Sylvain Grébaut in with funds of Jean Saint. The manuscripts originated from Šawā (ʾAddis ʾAbabā, Dabra Libānos, Dabra Bǝrhān), Goǧǧām (Dimā Giyorgis, Marṭula Māryām, Dabra Warq) and Bagemdǝr (Gondar, ʾAzazo Takla Hāymānot) and very few from Tǝgrāy.
history.acquisition: The manuscript entered the library on 18 April 1928.

bibl.title: Bybliothecae apostolicae Vaticanae codices manu scripti recensiti iussu Pii XI Pontificis maximi. Codices Aethiopici Vaticani et Borgiani, Barberinianus orientalis 2, Rossianus 865<hi rend="italics">, I:</hi> Enarratio codicum

Bybliothecae apostolicae Vaticanae codices manu scripti recensiti iussu Pii XI Pontificis maximi. Codices Aethiopici Vaticani et Borgiani, Barberinianus orientalis 2, Rossianus 865<hi rend="italics">, II:</hi> Prolegomena, Indices, Tabulae

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

revisionDesc.change: Added physical desc, updated content description
